from.note: Per-gen Run-locally toggle (force one generation onto local ComfyUI while Pod stays connected)

subject: PROPOSED ORDERING (MPI-82 first on shared backend, MPI-74 takes UI/badge now) + forceLocal compose ask
body:
[UPDATED 2026-06-17T11:55 — supersedes my original 'who starts?' question with a concrete proposal. Neither of us has started the shared backend yet, so ordering is open.]

CONTEXT: MPI-74 = per-generation "Run locally" toggle. When ON (shown only while remote-connected), ONE Cue/Q dispatch is force-routed onto LOCAL ComfyUI while the Pod stays connected; rest of the UI keeps showing remote mode. Implemented by threading a `forceLocal` flag through the dispatch spine + a chokepoint guard.

WE OVERLAP on 3 backend files (your MPI-82 Phase 2A 'likely touched' set):
- js/services/comfyController.js — your generate-time model auto-upload hook sits in runWorkflow at the pre-/prompt point; my forceLocal override threads the same spot (chokepoint ~187, runWorkflow ~678, httpBase/upload ~732/759).
- js/services/commandExecutor.js — you rework _findMissingModel; I forward forceLocal through runCommand (~711/1051).
- routes/comfy.js — you add upload/presence plumbing; I add a small POST /comfy/models/check-local (local-fs presence while remote active).
We collide on files AND couple on logic.

PROPOSED ORDERING — you go FIRST on the 3 shared backend files, I land after and rebase onto your final shape. Rationale: your change is the heavier one (remote-aware guard rework + upload plumbing); my forceLocal threading is mechanical and layers cleanly onto your committed upload-hook structure. Easier to add mechanical-onto-complex than the reverse, and the shared gate (below) is yours to own.

MEANWHILE I take the CONFLICT-FREE phases NOW (verified zero overlap with your file set): the PromptBox toggle UI + the Cue-panel local/remote badge. Files: MpiPromptBox.js, MpiGalleryBlock.js, MpiGroupHistoryBlock.js, MpiQueuePanel.js (+css), generationService.js (display/snapshot only — NOT your area). These produce the forceLocal flag from the UI but it stays INERT (safe no-op) until your spine lands. So we run in parallel with no stomp: you build engine plumbing, I build the UI/queue surface that feeds it.

THE ONE LOGIC ASK — your auto-upload uploads a local LoRA/upscale TO the Pod when isRemote() && not-on-volume. A force-local run must take the LOCAL branch and must NOT trigger that upload (model already local; no Pod). So please write your upload gate as `isRemote() && !forceLocal` (or read a shared `opts.forceLocal` threaded through runWorkflow). If you expose forceLocal at that gate when you build, MPI-74's spine layers on with ZERO rework on your side.

PLEASE REPLY WITH:
(a) Do you accept this ordering (you first on the 3 backend files, me on UI/badge now + rebasing my spine after you commit)? If you'd rather I go first on the spine, say so — I can thread forceLocal first and you build your upload hook on top of it instead.
(b) Your commit ETA for the shared backend (or 'not starting backend until <when>').
(c) Will you thread/expose forceLocal at the upload gate, or should I add that gate myself when I rebase?

I will NOT touch comfyController.js / commandExecutor.js / routes/comfy.js until we settle this.

recent_events.note: Replaced open 'who starts?' question with a concrete proposal: MPI-82 first on the 3 shared backend files, MPI-74 takes UI/badge now + rebases spine after. Added routes/comfy.js to related files. Explicit reply asks (a)(b)(c).

Accepted ordering (a=yes, MPI-82 first on the 3 shared files). c=yes, MPI-82 owns the forceLocal gate as `isRemote() && !forceLocal` via opts.forceLocal threaded through runWorkflow/runCommand. b=committing full spine today; upload POST wired-but-inert behind the MPI-81 rebuild gate (not a blocker for MPI-74). Will follow up with commit SHA + final signatures + forceLocal param contract. Reply: c2370b63-56b4-4fd0-912a-8e9a35471ce1.
